questions.question: What is the period of the function y = sin(2x)?
questions.explanation: The period of y = sin(bx) is 2π/b. Here b = 2, so period = 2π/2 = π.

questions.question: A right triangle has sides of length 5 and 12. What is the length of the hypotenuse?
questions.explanation: Use the Pythagorean theorem: c² = 5² + 12² = 25 + 144 = 169, so c = 13.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A = 30°, angle B = 60°, and side a = 10. Find side b using the Law of Sines.
questions.explanation: Law of Sines: a/sin(A) = b/sin(B). So 10/sin(30°) = b/sin(60°). Since sin(30°) = 1/2 and sin(60°) = √3/2, we get b = 10(√3/2)/(1/2) = 10√3.

questions.question: What is the amplitude of the function y = -3cos(x) + 2?
questions.explanation: The amplitude is the absolute value of the coefficient of the trigonometric function. Here the coefficient of cos(x) is -3, so amplitude = |-3| = 3.

questions.question: A triangle has sides a = 6, b = 8, and c = 10. Is this a right triangle? Justify briefly.
questions.explanation: Check if a² + b² = c²: 6² + 8² = 36 + 64 = 100 = 10². Since the Pythagorean theorem holds, it is a right triangle.

questions.question: Find the vertical shift of the function y = sin(x) - 4.
questions.explanation: The vertical shift is the constant term added or subtracted from the trigonometric function. Here the function shifts down by 4 units.
